capture.say: Mr. Grey! And the clever Miss Farthing. Do come in — you're just in time to be far too late.
Behold the Four O'Clock Device. At the top of the hour, every clock on Earth stops at 4:01. One minute PAST teatime. In perpetuity.
Thank you. The escapement is Swiss — nothing short of a sub-atomic jiffy of brine could trouble it. So do enjoy the lasers, and later, the not-tea.
Brine, you say. Baron, you really must stop monologuing. It's how they got Goldwhisk.

penny_grid.say: Swizzle stick, cross-phase the emitter, bish bosh. Hold my clipboard.
Lasers off. You may now menace the clock, Agent Tuxedo.

three59.say: One olive. Sub-atomically briny. With my compliments to the chef.
NO! Not a SAVOURY! It's a TEA, you PHILISTINE—
Escapement's jammed! It's stopping — it's stopping at... 3:59. Grey, it's ALWAYS almost teatime.

look.text: The emitter pylon sulks, cross-phased into a very expensive coat rack.
Rose-pink lasers, fanned out in front of the Device like a decorative and lethal doily.

use.text: It's off. Prodding it further would be gloating.
You reach out and reconsider at the last elegant moment. The lasers hum a warning in E flat.

itemUse.text: You hand Penny the swizzle stick and adopt a supervisory stance.
The olive is destined for greater things than laser trials. You feel this strongly.

look.text: Stopped at 3:59. The most beautiful minute-to in horological history.
A clock the size of a cathedral rose window, wired into a samovar the size of a small submarine. The hands crawl towards four.

use.text: The casing is Swiss, and smug about it. Brute force is not on the menu. Something briny is.
Between you and the Device: a fan of rose-pink lasers. Penny has opinions about lasers; perhaps consult her toolkit.

itemUse.text: You slot the silver pick into the escapement and post the olive through, brine-first.
You'd have to reach it first, and the lasers are between you and horological sabotage.

look.text: It steeps, ominously. The tea inside has been brewing since '61 and can probably be weaponised on its own.

use.text: You are not drinking sixty-five-year-old revenge tea. Even shaken.

look.text: Baron Marzipan: a cream suit with a man somewhere inside it, and a lapel rose made of actual marzipan.

itemUse.speaker: target
itemUse.text: Point that SAVOURY somewhere else, Mr. Grey.

exits.name: funicular
exits.look: The funicular waits, doors open. The Baron is a gracious host; it's the not-leaving-later you'd want to worry about.
